sections.heading: CNC turning service
sections.text: CNC turning service Source high-performing parts in more than 50 different metals and plastics via our network of advanced CNC turning experts. Tight tolerances available, down to Â±.0008" Get instant quote

sections.heading: What is CNC turning?
sections.text: What is CNC turning? CNC turning produces parts by mounting a blank on a rotating chuck and removing material using stationary cutting tools. This technology is ideal for manufacturing parts with symmetry along their center axis. Turned parts are typically produced faster (and at a lower cost) than milled parts. Learn more about CNC milling and turning

sections.heading: Maximum part size for CNC turning
sections.text: Maximum part size for CNC turning Part size limitations Metric units Imperial units Maximum part diameter 431 mm 17 in Maximum part length 990 mm 39 in Maximum swing over the carriage 350 mm 13.7 in Maximum spindle through-hole 40 mm 1.5 in Maximum speed: 1700RPM, Motor power: 640W

sections.heading: Available materials for CNC turning
sections.text: Available materials for CNC turning Industry-leading companies in aerospace, automotive, defense, machinery, medical technology, robotics, electronics, oil & gas, industrial automation and more that require high degrees of detail and complexity come to us for rapid prototypes and production parts. Metals A wide range of metals for CNC machining, with applications in multiple industries. Ideal for both one-off prototypes and end-use custom parts. Browse CNC turning metal materials Plastics A wide range of plastics for CNC machining, with applications in multiple industries. Ideal for both one-off prototypes and end-use custom parts. sections.heading: CNC turning surface finishes
sections.text: CNC turning surface finishes Enhance part performance by selecting surface finishes that improve the roughness, hardness, chemical resistance and cosmetic features of the finished component. Browse CNC turning surface finishes

sections.heading: CNC turning tolerancesÂ
sections.text: CNC turning tolerancesÂ We follow ISO 2768 standards for CNC machining. Limits for nominal size Plastics (ISO 2768- m) Metals (ISO 2768- f) 0.5mm* to 3mm Â±0.1mm Â±0.05mm Over 3mm to 6mm Â±0.1mm Â±0.05mm Over 6mm to 30mm Â±0.2mm Â±0.1mm Over 30mm to 120mm Â±0.3mm Â±0.15mm Over 120mm to 400mm Â±0.5mm Â±0.2mm Over 400mm to 1000mm Â±0.8mm Â±0.3mm Over 1000mm to 2000mm Â±1.2mm Â±0.5mm Over 2000mm to 4000mm Â±2mm *Please clearly indicate tolerances for nominal sizes below 0.5mm on your technical drawing.

sections.heading: We have high standards for CNC turning
sections.text: We have high standards for CNC turning We manufacture your custom parts according to strict manufacturing standards and ensure all parts and processes adhere to The Protolabs Network Standard . SeveralÂ surface finishes Â are available to improve the cosmetic quality of your parts after machining. Every custom part gets checked on site by our manufacturing partners according to our guidelines. Inspection reports and other types of quality documentation are available on request to assure the highest quality and compliance.
